title: PRD: Canvas Node & Connector Configuration
introduction: Today, demo.json is the single source of truth for an AnyDemo canvas, and the only UI-driven mutation we persist is node position (via PATCH /demos/:id/nodes/:nodeId/position). This feature widens UI editing so users can configure nodes and connectors directly on the canvas — text labels, descriptions, colors, edge style, arrow direction — and add purely-decorative shape nodes for annotation. A new bottom toolbar lets users drop annotation shapes with a click-drag interaction (Excalidraw-style), and connectors can be created by dragging from a node handle to another node. All edits write back to demo.json so they are diff-able, git-trackable, and survive across reloads.
goals: Let users edit a node's title and description inline on the canvas (double-click).
Let users style nodes (border color, background color) and connectors (color, line style, arrow direction) via a tabbed inspector sidebar.
Let users add purely-decorative shape nodes (rectangle, ellipse, sticky) by selecting a shape from a bottom toolbar and click-dragging on the canvas.
Let users create connectors between any two nodes by dragging from a source handle to a target handle.
Let users resize any node via React Flow's NodeResizer, persisting width/height to demo.json.
Let users delete a selected node or connector via the keyboard (Delete/Backspace) or a sidebar button, with cascade deletion of orphaned connectors when a node is removed.
Tighten the PlayNode header so the Play button is icon-only and the status pill only appears after the user has actually run the node.
Keep demo.json the single source of truth — every UI edit lands as an atomic, schema-validated write.

userStories.title: Schema additions: node visual fields + shapeNode type
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the demo schema to express node visual styling and a new annotation node type so the rest of the feature has a stable contract.
acceptanceCriteria: NodeDataBaseSchema gains optional width (positive number), height (positive number), borderColor (ColorTokenSchema), backgroundColor (ColorTokenSchema).
Inline-edited node description reuses the existing detail.summary field — no new 'description' field added.

New ShapeNodeSchema added to the discriminated union with type literal 'shapeNode' and data: { shape: 'rectangle' | 'ellipse' | 'sticky', label?: string }. Inherits the new visual fields from the base.
All existing fixtures and demo files continue to parse cleanly (every new field is optional).
schema.test.ts covers: parsing a shapeNode round-trip, accepting nodes with/without width+height, rejecting invalid color tokens.
bun run typecheck passes.
bun run lint passes.

userStories.title: Schema additions: connector visual fields + 'default' kind
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the connector schema to express visual style overrides and a no-semantics 'default' kind so user-drawn connectors don't carry phantom HTTP/event payloads.
acceptanceCriteria: ConnectorBaseShape gains optional style ('solid' | 'dashed' | 'dotted'), color (ColorTokenSchema), direction ('forward' | 'backward' | 'both', default 'forward').
New DefaultConnectorSchema added to the discriminatedUnion with kind literal 'default' (no semantic fields beyond the base).
connectorToEdge.ts: if connector.style is set, use it; else fall back to STYLE_BY_KIND[kind]. 'default' maps to solid in STYLE_BY_KIND.
connectorToEdge.ts: if direction === 'both', set both markerStart and markerEnd; if 'backward', set markerStart only and clear markerEnd; if 'forward'/absent, current behavior.
schema.test.ts covers: round-trip of a default connector, style overriding kind, direction variants.
All existing demo files continue to parse cleanly.
bun run typecheck passes.
bun run lint passes.

userStories.title: Color palette token system
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a curated palette token map so colors stay theme-aware and demo.json files store readable token names rather than hex strings.
acceptanceCriteria: ColorTokenSchema = z.enum(['default','slate','blue','green','amber','red','purple','pink']) declared once in schema.ts and re-used everywhere.

Frontend module apps/web/src/lib/color-tokens.ts exports a single source of truth: { [token]: { border: string, background: string, edge: string } } using Tailwind/shadcn CSS variables (works in light + dark).
Helper colorTokenStyle(token, kind: 'node' | 'edge') returns the right CSS values for the call site.
color-tokens.test.ts covers: every enum value has a mapping, the helper returns a non-empty string for each token.
bun run typecheck passes.
bun run lint passes.

userStories.title: Backend node CRUD endpoints
userStories.description: As the frontend, I need to PATCH/POST/DELETE nodes against the running studio so UI edits persist to demo.json atomically and safely.
acceptanceCriteria: PATCH /api/demos/:id/nodes/:nodeId accepts a partial body { position?, label?, detail?, borderColor?, backgroundColor?, width?, height?, ...shapeNode-only fields }; merges into the on-disk node; rewrites demo.json via existing writeFileAtomic + withDemoWriteLock; validates the resulting demo via DemoSchema before commit.

POST /api/demos/:id/nodes accepts a complete new node payload (id auto-generated server-side as 'node-' + crypto.randomUUID() if absent); appends to demo.nodes; same atomic-write + validation guarantees.
DELETE /api/demos/:id/nodes/:nodeId removes the node and cascade-removes every connector with source === nodeId || target === nodeId in the same atomic write.
All three endpoints return 400 on schema failure (with issues), 404 on unknown demoId/nodeId, 500 on write failure.
Existing PATCH /position endpoint is kept as a fast path (drag-stop persistence) — semantics unchanged.
api.test.ts covers: PATCH partial update, POST creates, DELETE cascades to connectors, schema failure returns 400, unknown id returns 404.
bun run typecheck passes.
bun test passes.
bun run lint passes.

userStories.title: Backend connector CRUD endpoints
userStories.description: As the frontend, I need to PATCH/POST/DELETE connectors against the running studio so UI-driven edge edits persist to demo.json.
acceptanceCriteria: PATCH /api/demos/:id/connectors/:connId accepts a partial body { label?, style?, color?, direction?, kind?, eventName?, queueName?, method?, url? }; merges into the on-disk connector; same atomic-write + schema validation as the node endpoints.

POST /api/demos/:id/connectors accepts a complete new connector (id auto-generated if absent), defaults kind to 'default' if absent, validates source+target reference existing nodes via the schema's superRefine.
DELETE /api/demos/:id/connectors/:connId removes the connector from demo.connectors.
All endpoints return 400/404/500 with the same shape as the node endpoints.
api.test.ts covers: each verb, schema failure path (e.g. POST referencing unknown node), kind-discriminated payload validation.
bun run typecheck passes.
bun test passes.
bun run lint passes.

userStories.title: Generalize positionOverrides → pendingOverrides
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need to apply optimistic edits for any node/connector field (not just position) and reconcile them against server echoes so the canvas stays smooth across SSE reloads.
acceptanceCriteria: New hook usePendingOverrides<T> in apps/web/src/hooks/ (or co-located) keyed by entity id, holding Partial<T> values.
DemoView holds two such hooks: one for nodes, one for connectors.
On user edit: set the override BEFORE firing the API call (optimistic).
On API success + subsequent demo:reload echo: prune any override key whose persisted value matches the server copy. Generalize the existing positionOverrides match logic.
On API failure: drop the override and surface a data-testid='edit-error-banner' message; log the error.
DemoCanvas merges sourceNodes + nodeOverrides at render time (just like the existing positionOverrides path).
Existing position-drag flow keeps working byte-for-byte (same UX).
use-pending-overrides.test.ts covers: set + prune-on-match, set + drop-on-error, multi-field merge.
bun run typecheck passes.
bun test passes.
bun run lint passes.

userStories.title: ShapeNode component (rectangle / ellipse / sticky)
userStories.description: As a user, I want shape nodes to render distinctively so I can use them as decoration / annotation alongside functional nodes.
acceptanceCriteria: New apps/web/src/components/nodes/shape-node.tsx with three visual variants driven by data.shape.
rectangle: rounded rect, transparent bg by default, themed border.
ellipse: rounded-full container, themed border.
sticky: rounded rect with default amber background and a slight rotation/shadow to read as a sticky note.
Renders a Handle on each side (top/right/bottom/left) so drag-to-connect works in any direction.
Honors data.borderColor and data.backgroundColor when set (via colorTokenStyle).
Honors data.width / data.height when set; otherwise falls back to a sensible default size.
Registered in DemoCanvas's nodeTypes object alongside playNode and stateNode (still a stable module-scope reference).
data-testid='shape-node' with data-shape attribute for tests.
bun run typecheck passes.
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill.

userStories.title: All-node resizing via NodeResizer
userStories.description: As a user, I want to resize any selected node by dragging its corners so I can fit my canvas to the content.
acceptanceCriteria: NodeResizer (from @xyflow/react) is rendered inside every custom node component (PlayNode, StateNode, ShapeNode), visible only when selected.
On resize-stop, DemoCanvas fires onNodeResize?(nodeId, { width, height }) → DemoView PATCHes /api/demos/:id/nodes/:nodeId with { width, height } and applies a pending override (per US-021).
Resize-while-dragging stays in React Flow's internal state — we only PATCH on resize-stop (mirrors the position drag pattern).
Minimum size enforced (e.g. 80x40) so users can't accidentally zero a node.
Functional nodes' internal layout (header, status pill, play button) gracefully reflows at narrow widths.
bun run typecheck passes.
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill.

userStories.title: Tabbed sidebar (Detail / Style)
userStories.description: As a user, I want the inspector sidebar to be tabbed so the existing detail content stays uncluttered while I get a dedicated place for visual edits.
acceptanceCriteria: DetailPanel restructures into shadcn Tabs with two triggers: 'Detail' and 'Style'.
Detail tab keeps the current content (summary, fields, dynamic source, run, recent events) — no behavior change for existing users.
Style tab is shown for both the selected NODE and selected CONNECTOR (panel widens to also drive a new ConnectorPanel mode keyed by selectedConnectorId).
Active tab persists during a single selection (collapses back to 'Detail' on a new selection).
Existing data-testids on the Detail tab keep working (current tests must pass unchanged).
New data-testid='detail-panel-tab-style' for the Style tab trigger.
bun run typecheck passes.
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill.

userStories.title: Style tab editors (colors, edge style, direction, delete)
userStories.description: As a user, I want explicit controls in the Style tab for every visual field on a node or connector, plus a delete action.
acceptanceCriteria: Node Style tab: two color-swatch rows (Border, Background) showing 8 palette tokens; clicking a swatch PATCHes the node and applies a pending override.
Connector Style tab: one color-swatch row (Color), an edge style dropdown (Auto / Solid / Dashed / Dotted), a 3-state segmented control for direction (→ / ← / ↔). Each change PATCHes the connector.
Each tab ends with a destructive Delete button (variant='destructive'); clicking it triggers DELETE /api/demos/:id/nodes/:nodeId or /connectors/:connId and clears the selection.
All controls have data-testid attributes for testing (e.g. 'style-tab-border-color-blue', 'style-tab-edge-style', 'style-tab-direction-both', 'style-tab-delete').
Style tab respects pending overrides — selecting a color shows the chosen swatch as 'active' immediately.
bun run typecheck passes.
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill.

userStories.title: Inline text editing (double-click)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to double-click any text on a node or connector to edit it in place so I don't have to dig into a sidebar for the most common edit.
acceptanceCriteria: Double-clicking a node title (label) or its description swaps the text for an inline <input> / <textarea> respectively, focused with content selected.
Double-clicking a connector's label triggers an inline editor positioned at the edge label.
Commit on Enter (or blur) → PATCH the field. Cancel on Escape → restore the previous value, no PATCH.
Persistence cadence: debounce a PATCH 400ms after the last keystroke, OR fire immediately on blur — whichever fires first.
While editing, React Flow drag/select on that node is disabled (prevents typing being interpreted as a drag).

Empty string for required fields (e.g. node label on functional nodes) reverts to the previous value with a subtle shake animation; empty is allowed for optional fields (description, connector label).
data-testid='inline-edit-input' with data-field for tests.
bun run typecheck passes.
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill.

userStories.title: Delete affordances (keyboard + sidebar button)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to remove a selected node or connector via the keyboard or a clear button so deletion is obvious and fast.
acceptanceCriteria: Pressing Delete or Backspace while a node OR a connector is selected (and no inline editor is active) triggers the corresponding DELETE endpoint.
The Style tab's Delete button (US-025) is the visual fallback for the same action.
Cascade behavior is server-driven (US-019): deleting a node also removes adjacent connectors in one atomic write.
After a successful delete, selectedNodeId/selectedConnectorId is cleared so the panel closes.
Keyboard listener is scoped to the canvas region (does not fire when focus is in an unrelated input or in the inline editor).
bun run typecheck passes.
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill.

userStories.title: Bottom toolbar with shape draw mode
userStories.description: As a user, I want a bottom toolbar where I can pick a shape and then drag on the canvas to create it at the size I want (Excalidraw model).
acceptanceCriteria: New apps/web/src/components/canvas-toolbar.tsx pinned to the bottom-center of the canvas with three icon buttons: Rectangle, Ellipse, Sticky note.
Clicking a shape button enters 'draw mode' for that shape — cursor changes to crosshair, button shows pressed state, ESC cancels.

In draw mode, mouse-down + drag on the canvas pane shows a ghost preview rectangle/ellipse at the dragged size; mouse-up commits a POST /nodes with type='shapeNode', the chosen shape, position, width, height, and a generated id.
Successful POST → server SSE demo:reload echoes the new node into demo and the override prunes; on failure, the ghost vanishes and an error banner is shown.
Drawing only fires when the initial mouse-down lands on the React Flow pane (not on an existing node/edge).
After commit (or ESC), draw mode exits automatically and the cursor returns to default.
data-testid='canvas-toolbar' with per-button data-testid='toolbar-shape-rectangle' etc.
bun run typecheck passes.
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill.

userStories.title: Connector creation via handle drag
userStories.description: As a user, I want to drag from a node's handle to another node's handle to create a connector between them.
acceptanceCriteria: DemoCanvas sets nodesConnectable={true} (currently false).
DemoCanvas wires React Flow's onConnect — when fired with { source, target }, DemoView POSTs /api/demos/:id/connectors with kind='default' and a generated id.
While the round-trip is in flight, an optimistic 'pending' connector is shown (uses pendingOverrides for connectors).
On schema failure (e.g. server says source/target invalid), the optimistic connector is dropped and an error banner is shown.
Connecting a node to itself is rejected client-side (no POST fires).
Connecting two nodes that already have a connector between them is allowed (multiple edges supported by the schema and React Flow).
bun run typecheck passes.
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill.

userStories.title: Icon-only Play button + status hidden until first run
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Play button to be a compact icon-only control and the status indicator to appear only after I've clicked Play, so the node header stays clean for nodes I'm not actively running.
acceptanceCriteria: PlayNode's Play button shows only the Play icon — no 'Play' or 'Running…' text. The button keeps an accessible label via aria-label and a title tooltip ('Play' / 'Running…' depending on state).
The button still reflects running state visually (e.g. spinner/icon swap, disabled appearance) without using a text label.
StatusPill is NOT rendered on a PlayNode in the never-run state (when no entry exists in the useNodeRuns map for that node).

Once the user clicks Play (and an entry lands in the runs map for that node), the StatusPill becomes visible and persists thereafter — showing running → done/error and continuing to display the last terminal status until the page is reloaded or the demo file changes.
StateNode's existing StatusPill behavior is unaffected — state nodes are not user-triggered, their pill remains driven by emit() events.
Existing data-testid='play-button' is preserved; the conditional StatusPill on PlayNode gets a data-testid='play-node-status' so tests can assert presence/absence cleanly.
Existing tests that asserted the 'Play' / 'Running…' text on the button are updated to assert the icon + aria-label instead.
bun run typecheck passes.
bun run lint passes.
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill.

functionalRequirements.text: demo.json schema accepts optional width, height, borderColor, backgroundColor on every node.
demo.json schema accepts a new node type 'shapeNode' with shape ∈ {rectangle, ellipse, sticky} and an optional label/description.
demo.json schema accepts optional style, color, direction on every connector and a new 'default' connector kind with no semantic payload.
Color values across the schema are constrained to a curated 8-token palette enum; tokens are mapped to actual CSS values in the frontend.
PATCH /api/demos/:id/nodes/:nodeId accepts partial node updates (position, label, detail, colors, dimensions); existing /position endpoint is preserved as a fast path.
POST /api/demos/:id/nodes appends a new node and DELETE /api/demos/:id/nodes/:nodeId removes the node + cascades any adjacent connectors in a single atomic write.
PATCH/POST/DELETE /api/demos/:id/connectors[/:connId] provide the corresponding CRUD for connectors; new connectors default to kind='default'.

Every write goes through withDemoWriteLock + writeFileAtomic and re-validates the whole demo via DemoSchema before commit; schema failure returns 400 with issues, unknown id returns 404, write failure returns 500.
The frontend keeps an optimistic pendingOverrides map per node and per connector; entries are pruned on a matching demo:reload echo and dropped on API error with a visible banner.
Inspector sidebar uses a Tabs container with Detail and Style tabs; the Detail tab preserves all current behavior; the Style tab applies to whichever entity (node or connector) is selected.
The Style tab provides palette swatches for border/background (nodes) or color (connectors), an edge style dropdown, a 3-state direction toggle (connectors), and a destructive Delete button.
Double-clicking a node title, node description, or connector label opens an inline editor; commit on Enter or blur (with 400ms idle debounce, blur wins), cancel on Escape.
Pressing Delete or Backspace while a node or connector is selected (and no inline editor is active) deletes the entity.
All nodes are resizable via NodeResizer when selected; resize-stop persists width/height to demo.json.

A bottom-pinned canvas toolbar exposes three shape buttons (rectangle, ellipse, sticky); clicking a button enters draw mode; click-drag on the canvas pane creates the shape at the dragged size; ESC cancels draw mode.
Dragging from a node's source handle to another node's target handle creates a connector with kind='default'; same-node connections are rejected client-side.

PlayNode's Play button is icon-only (no text label, accessible label via aria-label/title); StatusPill on PlayNode is hidden until the node has an entry in the runs map (i.e. the user has clicked Play at least once), after which it remains visible showing the latest run status until page reload or demo reload.

nonGoals: Undo / redo (the user can git-revert demo.json).
Free hex color picker (palette-only for v1).
Text-only shape variant (a sticky with no border + transparent bg can cover this later if needed).
Promoting a 'default' connector to 'http' / 'event' / 'queue' via the UI (kind change still requires editing demo.json).
Adding playNode or stateNode via the toolbar (functional nodes stay editor-driven for v1).
Copy / paste / duplicate of nodes or connectors.
Snap-to-grid or alignment guides on resize.
Persisting StatusPill visibility across page reloads (US-030 deliberately resets to hidden on reload — last-run state is recovered only when a new run happens or the demo's runs map is rehydrated).

ui: Reuse shadcn primitives already in apps/web/src/components/ui/ (Button, Sheet, Tabs).
Reuse the Sheet-based DetailPanel; widen it with Tabs rather than building a new panel.
Reuse the existing positionOverrides reconciliation pattern for the generalized pendingOverrides — it has the desired UX (no flicker) and is already proven against the SSE echo.
Reuse the shapeNode → React Flow custom node registration pattern (module-scope nodeTypes object) so DemoCanvas stays idiomatic.
All new selectors / colors should respect the existing theme variables — no hard-coded hex outside color-tokens.ts.
filesToCreate: apps/web/src/components/inline-edit.tsx (or co-located inside each node component)
apps/studio/src/api.test.ts new specs for nodes + connectors CRUD (extend the existing file)
apps/studio/src/schema.test.ts new specs for shapeNode + default connector + visual fields
filesToModify: apps/web/src/App.tsx (or wherever DemoView lives — add selectedConnectorId state and wire onConnect)

technicalConsiderations.persistence: All UI-driven mutations go through new endpoints that share the per-demo write lock and atomic-write helpers in apps/studio/src/api.ts. Validation runs on the WHOLE demo (after the mutation) before the file is overwritten — never partial writes.
technicalConsiderations.watcherEcho: Each successful write triggers the existing fs watcher → broadcasts demo:reload over SSE → frontend re-fetches and prunes matching pendingOverrides. The demo:reload-driven reconciliation is the only place optimistic state is reconciled with disk truth.
technicalConsiderations.schemaCompatibility: Every new schema field is optional. Existing demo.json files (e.g. examples/todo-demo-target/.anydemo/demo.json) must continue to parse with no edits.
technicalConsiderations.performance: Inline-edit debounce (400ms idle) + blur-wins prevents one PATCH per keystroke. Position drag and resize drag stay in React Flow internal state during the gesture and only PATCH on stop (mirrors the existing US-014 pattern).
technicalConsiderations.testing: Backend: extend api.test.ts with the new endpoints (happy path + schema failure + cascade-delete + unknown id). Frontend: hooks (use-pending-overrides) get unit tests; UI stories include 'Verify in browser using dev-browser skill' as acceptance criteria. End-to-end is not formally part of the PRD but smoke-testing in the studio's prod-mode bundle is recommended.

successMetrics: A user can add a sticky note explaining a service node, color it amber, draw a default connector to the service node, and edit the connector label — entirely from the UI, with all changes round-tripping cleanly to demo.json.
Existing demos (examples/todo-demo-target) load and behave identically without any edits.
All UI mutations land in demo.json within 500ms of the gesture (excluding the 400ms inline-edit debounce).
No flicker, snap-back, or duplicate-node artifacts during the SSE echo of any UI-driven mutation.
Schema validation rejects malformed POST/PATCH bodies with clear 400 responses (no half-written demo.json files).
The PlayNode header reads cleanly at default size — no text on the Play button, no status pill on never-run nodes.
openQuestions: Should cascade-deleting a node show a confirmation dialog when N adjacent connectors will be removed (e.g. N >= 3), or is a silent cascade fine for v1?

Should the shape draw-mode preview (ghost rectangle/ellipse) be the React Flow native pattern (a 'preview' node), or a plain absolute-positioned div on top of the canvas? The latter is simpler but skips React Flow's coordinate transforms.

Sticky notes default to amber background — should the user-applied backgroundColor token replace the amber default entirely, or layer on top? (Recommendation: replace, so the backgroundColor field is authoritative.)

Inline editor for a connector label: where exactly does it render? React Flow's edge labels are inside an <svg>, so the editor probably needs an absolute-positioned overlay using the edge's labelX/labelY.

US-030: when a Play node is mid-run (running) and the user reloads the page, should the status pill show 'running' or stay hidden until the next emit() event lands? (Likely hidden — pure session state — but worth confirming.)

decidedDuringBrainstorming: Persistence target: A — everything writes to demo.json.
UX surface: mix — inline for text, tabbed sidebar for colors/style.
Connector style/kind: A — optional 'style' field overrides kind-derived default.
Arrow direction: C — 3 states (forward / backward / both).
Color picker: A — curated palette of token names, no hex.
Add-node flow: shapeNode-only via toolbar; Excalidraw click-drag; all nodes resizable.
PlayNode header: icon-only Play button, status pill hidden until first run.
